stimulus: The Graham technique, pioneered by American dancer and choreographer Martha Graham, is grounded in a principle called contraction and release, a rhythmic alternation between tension and ease in the torso. The principle pervades the entire body of the dance ______ performers use it to convey emotions ranging from grief to euphoria within a single phrase.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The convention being tested is the use of punctuation between two main clauses. A semicolon is conventionally used to join the first main clause ("The principle...dance") with the second main clause ("performers...phrase").

Choice B is incorrect because it produces a run-on sentence: the two main clauses are fused without any punctuation or coordinating conjunction. Choice C is incorrect because a comma alone cannot join two main clauses, producing a comma splice. Choice D is incorrect because the relative pronoun "which" cannot be used in this way to link two independent clauses.
